summary: Parallel review: FAIL (1 critical, 9 warning, 12 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 `bin/lib/cron.mjs:115` — `setProjectItemStatus` return value silently discarded on the "in-progress" transition. If the API call fails, dispatch proceeds on an item still marked "Ready" on the board, creating a race window in multi-machine setups. Add a warning log.

🟡 `artifacts/test-output.txt` — Stale artifact. Contains test name "logs a warning when setProjectItemStatus returns false but continues" which no longer exists in the current test file (replaced by "prefixes log lines with ISO timestamp"). Artifact should reflect final code state.

🟡 `bin/lib/cron.mjs:122-126` — Recovery operations in catch block (`_setProjectItemStatus`, `_commentIssue`) are not try/catch wrapped. If either throws, the original error is silently lost and `tsError` never executes.
🟡 `bin/lib/cron.mjs:115,120` — Return value of `_setProjectItemStatus` is discarded on both happy and error paths. Board can desynchronize silently if the GitHub API fails.
| 🟡 | `--interval > 59` produces invalid cron without warning (user gets hourly instead of expected frequency) | SPEC.md:8 / cron.mjs:142 |
| 🟡 | Silent failure in catch block — if recovery ops throw, original error is lost from cron.log | cron.mjs:122-126 |
- Builder fixed all 3 previous review findings: dead `existsSync` import (🔴), missing exit code assertion (🟡), missing lock release spy (🟡)
🟡 `bin/lib/cron.mjs:96` — No guard against non-array return from `listProjectItems`; `items.filter()` would throw TypeError if dependency returns null/undefined

🟡 `bin/lib/cron.mjs:110` — Title sanitization regex misses Unicode line separators (U+2028, U+2029, U+0085). Low practical risk given GitHub normalization and the human-approval gate, but should be expanded for defense-in-depth.
🟡 `bin/lib/cron.mjs:125` — Error messages from `runSingleFeature` posted verbatim to GitHub issue comments could expose internal paths on public repos. Consider truncating.
